symptoms.symptom_name: Severe colicky abdominal pain in waves

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colicky, wave-like pain reflects peristalsis against an obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent large-volume bilious vomiting; unable to tolerate fluids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilious vomiting indicates obstruction with proximal build-up; also drives dehydration.
Vomiting without any diarrhoea and with distension/absolute constipation argues against gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absolute constipation — no flatus or stool for two days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure to pass flatus and stool is the cardinal feature of complete mechanical obstruction.
Distension from proximal bowel dilatation; more marked in distal/large-bowel obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Insidious change in bowel habit to persistent constipation over four months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive change in bowel habit in an older adult is a cardinal colorectal-cancer red flag.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent bright red rectal bleeding mixed with stool

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Blood mixed with stool suggests a colonic (rather than purely anal) source; a key red flag.

symptoms.symptom_name: Significant unintentional weight loss (~8 kg) with anorexia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unexplained weight loss is a constitutional cancer red flag.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tenesmus / sensation of incomplete rectal emptying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tenesmus suggests a distal (rectosigmoid) mass lesion.
Fatigue is consistent with chronic anaemia from occult GI blood loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Not taking opioid or anti-motility/constipating medication

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excludes a simple drug-induced explanation for the constipation, strengthening a pathological obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: High-pitched 'tinkling', hyperactive bowel sounds on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tinkling hyperactive bowel sounds are characteristic of mechanical obstruction — sealed clinician auscultation, not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable firm, fixed left-lower-quadrant abdominal mass with tympanic percussion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A discrete fixed mass indicates the obstructing tumour — sealed clinician palpation, not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Empty rectal vault with streaks of altered blood on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An empty vault excludes simple impaction and points to a higher obstructing lesion — sealed clinician DRE.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR ~110) with pale, diaphoretic, cachectic appearance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia reflects hypovolaemia/pain; cachexia reflects malignancy — measured vitals are sealed clinician data.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT abdomen/pelvis showing large bowel obstruction with an obstructing mass

investigation_details.expected_result: Dilated proximal large bowel to the point of an obstructing left-colon lesion; assess for perforation/ischaemia.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed obstruction level and cause; delayed surgical intervention.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CT confirms the obstruction, its cause and any perforation — hospital investigation, not available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Colonoscopy showing an irregular, friable, ulcerating colorectal mass

investigation_details.expected_result: Irregular friable ulcerating mass; biopsy for histological confirmation of malignancy.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: No tissue diagnosis; but not appropriate acutely during complete obstruction.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation and biopsy confirm the tumour — the anticipated diagnostic finding, never available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low haemoglobin with iron-deficiency indices on full blood examination and iron studies

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood examination and iron studies
investigation_details.expected_result: Microcytic hypochromic anaemia with low ferritin/transferrin saturation consistent with chronic occult GI blood loss.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed anaemia severity requiring transfusion and correction before surgery.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms iron-deficiency anaemia from occult colonic blood loss — hospital bloods, not available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Colicky pain + vomiting + absolute constipation + distension together define a surgical-emergency obstruction.
Change in bowel habit + rectal bleeding + weight loss + anaemia in an older adult is a fast-track cancer-referral pattern that reframes the acute obstruction as malignant.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency department assessment with CT abdomen/pelvis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the obstruction, its level and cause and screens for perforation/ischaemia — the AI's role is to route to ED where this is done, not to order it remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — FBE, iron studies, U&E/creatinine, lactate, group and hold
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies anaemia, dehydration, electrolyte/renal derangement and prepares for surgery — hospital investigations.
Contraindicated acutely during complete obstruction; deferred to after decompression — the AI should not recommend it in the acute setting.
Attempting to relieve the constipation remotely is dangerous in mechanical obstruction and delays surgical care.
